blocks.body: Word Order is one of the most important aspects of English grammar. Unlike Ukrainian, where word order is relatively free, English requires a clear sequence of words in a sentence to convey the correct meaning.

• S–V–O — subject, verb, object: She reads books.
  • Adverbs of frequency before the main verb: I always drink coffee.
  • Adverbials of place and time at the end (Place → Time): She works at home every day.
  • Formula: S + (Adv) + V + O + Place + Time.
blocks.heading: Basic word order

blocks.body:
• Yes/No questions: Do you like pizza?
  • Wh-question: Where do you live?
  • Disclaimer: I don't like apples.
  • Helping verbs: do/does/did + not.
blocks.heading: Questions and Objections

blocks.body:
• Adverbs of frequency (always, often, never) — before the verb: She always arrives early.
  • Adverbs of action (quickly, well) — at the end of the sentence: He speaks English fluently.
  • Order of adverbials: Manner → Place → Time.
blocks.heading: Adverbs and Adverbials

blocks.body:
Topic Level Description
Basic Word Order A1 The basic structure of S–V–O in statements
Questions & Negatives A1–A2 Questions and negatives with do/does/did
Adverbs & Adverbials A2–B1 The position of adverbs and adverbials
Verbs & Objects A2–B1 Modal and phrasal verbs
Advanced Emphasis B1–B2 Inversion and cleft sentences
blocks.heading: Topics in this topic

blocks.body:
• Inversion — with negative adverbs: Never have I seen...
  • It-cleft — reinforcement of the element: It was you who called.
  • What-cleft - reinforcement of action: What I need is rest.
  • Emphatic do — reinforcement of the statement: I do like it!
blocks.heading: Advanced structures

blocks.body:
🧠

Start with the basic word order S–V–O is the basis of an English sentence.

Then study the questions and negatives with do/does/did.

Adverbs of frequency are placed before the verb, and adverbs of manner of action are at the end.

For an advanced level, inversion and cleft sentences add formality and emphasis.
blocks.heading: Learning tips
